title: The Amazon Rainforest
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The Amazon rainforest is often (0) to as 'the lungs of the Earth' because it produces approximately twenty percent of the world's oxygen. This vast ecosystem (1) an area of over five million square kilometres and is home to an astonishing variety of wildlife. Scientists believe that one in ten known species on Earth (2) in the Amazon. (3) this precious habitat is under serious threat from deforestation. Every year, thousands of hectares of forest are (4) down to make way for agriculture and cattle ranching. Environmental groups have been (5) for stricter regulations to protect the remaining forest. Indigenous communities, who have lived in harmony with the forest for centuries, are also (6) out against the destruction of their homeland. Many experts argue that preserving the Amazon is essential in the (7) against climate change. (8) urgent action, we risk losing one of the most biodiverse regions on the planet forever.

example.explanation: 'Referred to as' is the correct expression to indicate how something is commonly called.

questions.explanation: 'Covers an area' is the correct collocation to indicate geographical extent.
'Lives in' is the natural verb to indicate where species inhabit.
'However' introduces a contrast and is followed by a comma at the start of a sentence.
'Cut down' is the correct phrasal verb for felling trees.
'Calling for' is the appropriate phrasal verb to demand or publicly request something.
'Speaking out against' (con 'out against' ya presente en el texto) significa expresarse públicamente en contra de algo.
'The fight against' is the most natural collocation for the struggle against a problem.
'Without' indica la ausencia de algo necesario para evitar un resultado negativo.
